message:
fix(ci): use flock to serialize zig CC calls per target

The per-target cache dir approach failed because Go parallel package
compilation spawns multiple zig CC processes within a single goreleaser
target, racing on the zig cache. Using flock serializes zig invocations
per target, preventing AccessDenied errors in gozstd compilation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/crew/emma
Rig: beads
Role: crew

files.patch:
@@ -50,13 +50,10 @@ jobs:
             "cc-x86_64-freebsd:x86_64-freebsd"; do
             name="${pair%%:*}"
             target="${pair##*:}"
-            # Each wrapper gets its own zig cache to avoid AccessDenied races
-            # when goreleaser builds multiple targets in parallel.
-            # Must unset first because repo/workflow env vars override exports.
-            cache_dir="/tmp/zigcc/cache-${target}"
-            mkdir -p "$cache_dir"
-            printf '#!/bin/sh\nunset ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR\nexport ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR=%s\nexport ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR=%s\nexec zig cc -target %s "$@"\n' \
-              "$cache_dir" "$cache_dir" "$target" > "/tmp/zigcc/$name"
+            # Serialize zig calls per target with flock to prevent
+            # AccessDenied races when go build invokes CC in parallel.
+            printf '#!/bin/sh\nexec flock /tmp/zigcc/lock-%s zig cc -target %s "$@"\n' \
+              "$target" "$target" > "/tmp/zigcc/$name"
             chmod +x "/tmp/zigcc/$name"
           done
